channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: What to know about Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro and the alleged arson at his official residence
item.description: A man is facing charges after authorities say he broke into the Pennsylvania governor’s mansion in the middle of the night, set a fire that left significant damage and forced Gov. Josh Shapiro, his family and guests to evacuate the building during the Jewish holiday of Passover.
item.dc:creator: Marc Levy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Retrial opens for Sarah Palin's libel suit against The New York Times
item.description: A retrial is set to begin for Sarah Palin's lawsuit accusing The New York Times of libeling her in an editorial eight years ago.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: President Trump says CBS and '60 Minutes' should 'pay a big price' for going after him
item.description: President Donald Trump bitterly attacked “60 Minutes” shortly after the CBS newsmagazine broadcast stories on Ukraine and Greenland on Sunday, saying the network was out of control and should “pay a big price” for going after him.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump is 'fully fit' to serve as commander in chief, his doctor says after recent physical
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Donald Trump’s doctor says the oldest man elected president is “fully fit” to serve as commander in chief as the White House released results Sunday of Trump's recent physical exam.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Bill de Blasio on what's next for the Democratic Party
item.description: De Blasio, who has received fierce criticism from President Trump this week, believes the Democrats blew the presidential election on multiple fronts.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Trump takes swipe at Biden, says US will support Somalia against Houthis
item.description: President Trump warned Houthis to hide after removing red tape and empowering the U.S. military to protect the people of Somalia from terrorists.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Results of President Trump's physical released, here's what they say
item.description: President Trump is in "excellent health," the White House physician said in a release following the president's annual physical at Walter Reed National Military Medical Center.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump team tries to project confidence and calm after his tariff moves rattled markets
item.description: White House advisers and Cabinet members are trying to project confidence and calm as they defend President Donald Trump’s economic policies after another week of reeling markets that saw the Republican administration reverse course on some of its steepest tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: White House doctor says Trump is 'fully fit' in medical report
item.description: President Trump lost 20 pounds, lowered “bad” cholesterol and has “diverticulosis and a benign polyp,” according to a medical report released by the White House.
item.dc:creator: Luke Garrett, MPR News

item.title: Video of Trump allegedly 'snubbing' actress Cheryl Hines spreads like wildfire but doesn't show full picture
item.description: President Donald Trump circled back to greet Cheryl Hines despite recent headlines claiming the president snubbed Secretary Kennedy's wife at a UFC fight.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: SCOOP: Arizona to begin removing as many as 50K noncitizens from voter rolls following lawsuit
item.description: Arizona will begin removing noncitizens from its voter rolls after a successful lawsuit by America First Legal paved the way for counties to coordinate with DHS.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: State Department wants staff to report instances of alleged anti-Christian bias during Biden's term
item.description: As the Christian world commemorates Holy Week leading up to Easter Sunday, the State Department has issued an appeal for its employees to report instances of alleged anti-Christian bias, including actions taken for opposition to vaccines or personal pronoun choice, that may have occurred during form
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Warren demands SEC investigate Trump for insider trading, accuses him of unleashing 'chaos' with tariffs
item.description: Massachusetts Democratic Sen. Elizabeth Warren doubled down on her call for the SEC to investigate President Trump over alleged market manipulation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: PETA, animal rights groups praise Trump admin for phasing out 'cruel tests on dogs' and other animals
item.description: PETA and other animal advocacy groups have praised the Trump administration for recent announcements to phase out animal testing at the FDA and EPA.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump admin tells judge man wrongly deported to El Salvador is alive, still detained
item.description: On Friday, federal judge Paula Xinis had ordered the Trump administration to provide daily updates to return Kilmar Armando Abrego Garcia to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster, MPR News

item.title: Iran and US envoys hold negotiation over Tehran's nuclear program
item.description: Iran's state-run broadcaster revealed that U.S. Mideast envoy Steve Witkoff and Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi “briefly spoke" together.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: White House authorizes military control of land along the border
item.description: In a historic move, the Department of Defense now has control of the Roosevelt Reservation, a 60-foot-wide federal strip.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Trump orders military to take control of federal land at southern border
item.description: President Trump in a memorandum Friday ordered the military to take control of a strip of federal land along the Southern border that would be designated "National Defense Areas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Maher praises ‘gracious and measured’ Trump after White House visit
item.description: The talk show host emphasized that he did not turn “MAGA” during the meeting over dinner at the White House on Mar. 31.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration ends temporary protected status for thousands of Afghans amid deportation push
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security doesn't plan to renew the protected status of thousands of Afghans who fled the country in 2021, when the U.S. withdrew and the Taliban took over.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court gives Trump wiggle room with mistaken deportation decision
item.description: A Supreme Court decision pushing for the return of a man mistakenly deported to a Salvadoran prison has both his lawyers and the Trump administration claiming victory, leaving unclear if and when Kilmar Abrego Garcia will return home.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration says it will exclude some electronics from 'reciprocal' tariffs
item.description: The Trump administration says it will exclude electronics like smartphones and laptops from ‘reciprocal’ tariffs, a move that could help keep the prices down for popular consumer electronics that aren’t usually made in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Here's what happened during Trump's 12th week in office
item.description: Trump announced the U.S. would lower reciprocal tariffs for other countries, while also revealing that his administration would immediately hike tariffs on Chinese goods to 145%.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China put steep tariffs on U.S. exports. Farmers are worried
item.description: The first Trump administration spent $28 billion bailing out farmers during a trade war with China. The White House has said it's starting to look at how to help this time around.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben, MPR News

item.title: Democrats dislike the 'chaos' of Trump's trade war but are OK with some tariffs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Democrats are quick to say that President Donald Trump’s tariffs are horrible, awful, terrible. But Democrats are also stressing that they are not inherently anti-tariff. What Trump's political opponents say they really dislike is the “chaos” he has unleashed.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: K-12 schools face major Trump test on DEI demand
item.description: If the programs are not gone, the administration says, funding could be pulled from the K-12 districts.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: GOP frustrations with conservatives rise after budget fight
item.description: Frustrations are bubbling up among House Republicans after the conference — by the skin of its teeth — overcame internal disputes to adopt a framework for President Trump’s legislative agenda, a troubling sign for the group as it heads into the next, more difficult, step in achieving the president’s
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Longtime House Republican weighs run to flip Dem-held Senate seat in key battleground
item.description: Longtime Republican Rep. Bill Huizenga of Michigan is weighing a run in the 2026 midterm elections for Michigan's open Senate seat, which the GOP is aiming to flip.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Washington, DC, faces budget crisis after House leaves $1.1 billion hole in city coffers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — When the House of Representatives adjourned for a two-week recess Thursday, it left Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bondi appoints new interim U.S. Attorney for San Diego, Imperial counties
item.description: The Southern District of California will have a new U.S attorney after Attorney General Pam Bondi appointed a longtime San Diego prosecutor to take over the role as the region's top federal law enforcement official following Tara McGrath's termination.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Immigration judge allows Mahmoud Khalil deportation effort to proceed
item.description: A judge in former Columbia University activist Mahmoud Khalil's case ruled against him Friday, finding he is eligible for deportation while giving his legal team a little less than two weeks to respond.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about Dan Caine, the nation's newly-confirmed top military adviser
item.description: The Senate voted around 2 a.m. Friday to confirm retired Air Force Lt. Gen. Dan Caine as chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff. Trump plucked him from retirement to be his top military adviser.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, Juliana Kim, and Tom Bowman, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court rules Trump admin must bring Kilmar Abrego Garcia back to US
item.description: The U.S. Supreme Court upheld on Thursday a judge's order requiring President Donald Trump's administration to facilitate the return to the United States of a Salvadoran man who the government has acknowledged was deported in error to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: By John Kruzel and Andrew Chung / Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota Department of Health workers rally against Trump administration cuts
item.description: Members of the Minnesota Association of Professional Employees rallied outside a state office building on the grounds of the State Capitol Thursday to speak out against cuts to public health programs.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Federal jobs watch: Just-released February jobs report shows few losses in Minnesota
item.description: The federal workforce includes nearly 34,000 jobs in Minnesota. Over 20,000 are located in the Twin Cities metropolitan area. The Bureau of Labor Statistics’ month jobs report shows a loss of 100 federal jobs through Minnesota from January to February.
item.dc:creator: Elisabeth Gawthrop and Craig Helmstetter, MPR News

item.title: Trump must face Central Park 5 defamation suit, judge rules
item.description: A federal judge declined to toss a defamation lawsuit against President Trump over his remarks last year about five Black and Hispanic men wrongly convicted of a 1989 rape, dubbed the Central Park Five. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Public bathroom access in NYC to be voted on by City Council
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Knowing where the closest bathroom is in New York City can be a challenge, but a new City Council bill may change that. Councilmember Sandy Nurse is leading the push to expand public bathroom access.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11
